description:
## Goal

Add `spec/monitoring_spec.cr` covering the public API of
`RefreshLoop::Monitoring` (the merged module from the previous
`quickhea-pow` ticket).

## Why

The merge unified two previously-tested modules
(`RefreshHealthMonitor` and `HealthReporter`) into a single
module that has no spec. Before the merge:
- `HealthReporter` had a 4-example spec
  (`spec/refresh_health_reporter_spec.cr`, deleted in the merge)
- `RefreshHealthMonitor` was only tested transitively through
  the `Supervisor` spec

After the merge, **0 examples** exercise `Monitoring`'s
public API directly. The merge is supposed to be behavior-
preserving, but with no spec we have no regression test for
"the merge preserved behavior" — a future refactor could
silently change the contract.

This was flagged in the code review as the **must-fix** item
from the 5 recent commits.

## Coverage

Minimum (1 spec file, ~15 examples):

- `REPORT_INTERVAL` constant value
- State tracking:
  - `record_cycle_start` sets `@@last_refresh_start`
  - `record_cycle_complete` sets `@@last_refresh_complete`
    and increments `@@refresh_cycles_completed`
  - `record_failure` increments `@@refresh_failures`
  - `reset_failures` zeroes `@@refresh_failures`
  - `feed_fetch_started` increments `@@feeds_in_progress`
  - `feed_fetch_completed` decrements (and clamps at 0)
- `status` returns the expected named-tuple shape and values
- `stuck?`:
  - returns `false` when never started (`@@last_refresh_start == 0`)
  - returns `false` when `last_complete > start_time`
  - returns `true` when age exceeds `max_age_seconds`
  - returns `false` when age is within `max_age_seconds`
- `attempt_recovery`:
  - returns `true` and zeros `@@last_refresh_start` when stuck
  - returns `false` when not stuck
  - returns `false` when never started
- `start` returns without raising, is safe to call multiple
  times
- `last_refresh_start_for_testing=` /
  `last_refresh_complete_for_testing=` setters (used by
  `dev_tools/refresh_simulator.cr`)

## Out of scope

- `report_status` (private) reads from 4 actors
  (`MemoryManagerActor`, `SocketManager`,
  `EventBroadcaster`, `FiberTracker`). Mocking all 4 to test the
  full body is significant scaffolding. Defer to
  `quickhea-X3` (separate ticket, "Improve testability of
  Monitoring.report_status").

## Acceptance

- `spec/monitoring_spec.cr` exists and follows the existing
  conventions
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec spec/monitoring_spec.cr`
  passes
- Full suite (231+ examples) still passes
- `nix develop . --command ameba spec/monitoring_spec.cr`
  is clean
- `nix develop . --command just nix-build` succeeds
title: Add spec/monitoring_spec.cr for RefreshLoop::Monitoring
